title: BAI Implements 3x15 Scoring System for Domestic Junior Tournaments
indian_angle: This new scoring system will be implemented across all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships for junior categories in India.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has announced the implementation of a new 3x15 scoring system for domestic ranking tournaments and national championships in the U-13, U-15, and U-17 age groups.

title: India's New 3x15 Scoring System to Debut at Ernakulam Tournament
indian_angle: The new 3x15 scoring system, set to revolutionize junior domestic badminton in India, will officially debut at a tournament in Ernakulam.
summary: India's domestic badminton circuit will adopt a new 3x15 scoring system across all tournaments, with its first official implementation scheduled for an event in Ernakulam.

title: Kidambi Srikanth Finishes Runner-Up at US Open Men's Singles Final
indian_angle: Indian shuttler Kidambi Srikanth reached the final of the US Open but was unable to secure the title, finishing as runner-up.
summary: Kidambi Srikanth's impressive run at the US Open concluded with a loss in the final, where he faltered at the last hurdle against Su Li Yang.

title: Indian Shuttler Rounak Chouhan Upsets World No. 6 at US Open
indian_angle: Young Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan caused a major upset at the US Open by defeating the World No. 6, Chou Tien Chen.
summary: Indian badminton player Rounak Chouhan made headlines at the US Open Super 300 by stunning World No. 6 Chou Tien Chen.

title: Asian Games Squad Reassembles; Treesa-Gayatri Return to Indian Team
indian_angle: The Indian badminton squad, including the returning Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand, is reassembling to prepare for the upcoming Asian Games.
summary: The Indian badminton contingent that competed in the Thomas Cup is reconvening for preparations ahead of the Asian Games, with the notable return of the women's doubles pair Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand.

title: Satwik-Chirag Crowned Singapore Open Men's Doubles Champions
indian_angle: India's star men's doubles pair, Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty, secured a prestigious title at the Singapore Open.
summary: Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty clinched the Singapore Open men's doubles title after a display of furious rallies, powerful smashes, and celebratory dances.
